# Test Highlights

This document is designed for testing highlight annotations.

## Highlight Test 1: Single Color

**Instructions**: Use the highlight tool to highlight the text below with yellow:

<!-- ANNOTATED: 1 highlight, 1 stroke -->
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

## Highlight Test 2: Multiple Colors

**Instructions**: Highlight each word with a different color:

<!-- ANNOTATED: 1 highlight -->
- Red highlight: important
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

<!-- ANNOTATED: 1 highlight -->
- Yellow highlight: critical
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

<!-- ANNOTATED: 1 highlight -->
- Green highlight: note
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

<!-- ANNOTATED: 1 highlight -->
- Blue highlight: warning
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

## Highlight Test 3: Overlapping Highlights

**Instructions**: Create overlapping highlights on the text below:

<!-- ANNOTATED: 4 highlights -->
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

## Highlight Test 4: Long Passage

**Instructions**: Highlight key phrases in this passage:

<!-- ANNOTATED: 3 highlights -->
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This pangram contains every letter of the alphabet. It is commonly used for testing and displaying fonts. Many designers and developers use this phrase regularly.
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

---

End of highlight tests.
